lesson.menu_desc: Professional Emails, Formal Register and Hedging Language -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- Emails That Get Results -- Formal Register and Hedging
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 5 -- Emails That Get Results

listenings.text: Hi, this is Walyson. I am recording a quick note about an email I am sending to our supplier in Colombia. The subject line is short and clear: Request for an updated price list. I open the email politely, so I write: I am writing regarding our order for next month. Then I make my request in a soft way. I write: I was wondering if you could send me the updated price list and confirm the delivery date. I also add one document, so I write: please find the contract in the attachment. Before I send it, I read my draft one more time and I check the recipient at the top. At the end, I ask them to acknowledge the email and to reply by Friday. I always close with a polite line: I would appreciate a quick answer. Best regards, Walyson.

Hi Walyson, this is Marina in Geneva. I am calling to follow up on your email regarding the new contract. First, thank you for your message and for the attachment. I can confirm that I received the draft, and everything looks clear. I have only one small question: could you clarify the payment terms in section three? I was also wondering if you could forward the email to our finance team, so they can reply directly. I would appreciate it if we could confirm everything by Friday. If anything is not clear, please reach out to me. I will reply to your email today with my comments. Thank you again, and talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, write a short and clear subject line. Next, open the email politely and say what it is regarding. Then make your request in a soft way, using could you or I was wondering if. After that, mention the attachment and ask the recipient to reply. Finally, close with a polite line and your name.
